speaker: NERISSA
text: Quick, quick, I pray thee, draw the curtain straight.

speaker: NERISSA
text: The Prince of Arragon hath ta’en his oath

speaker: NERISSA
text: And comes to his election presently.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Behold, there stand the caskets, noble prince.

speaker: PORTIA
text: If you choose that wherein I am contained,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Straight shall our nuptial rites be solemnized.

speaker: PORTIA
text: But if you fail, without more speech, my lord,

speaker: PORTIA
text: You must be gone from hence immediately.

speaker: ARRAGON
text: I am enjoined by oath to observe three things:

speaker: ARRAGON
text: First, never to unfold to anyone

speaker: ARRAGON
text: Which casket ’twas I chose; next, if I fail

speaker: ARRAGON
text: Of the right casket, never in my life

speaker: ARRAGON
text: To woo a maid in way of marriage;

speaker: ARRAGON
text: Lastly, if I do fail in fortune of my choice,

speaker: ARRAGON
text: Immediately to leave you, and be gone.

speaker: PORTIA
text: To these injunctions everyone doth swear

speaker: PORTIA
text: That comes to hazard for my worthless self.

speaker: ARRAGON
text: And so have I addressed me. Fortune now

speaker: ARRAGON
text: To my heart’s hope! Gold, silver, and base lead.

speaker: ARRAGON
text: “Who chooseth me must give and hazard all he hath.”

speaker: ARRAGON
text: You shall look fairer ere I give or hazard.

speaker: ARRAGON
text: What says the golden chest? Ha, let me see:

speaker: ARRAGON
text: “Who chooseth me shall gain what many men desire.”

speaker: ARRAGON
text: What many men desire—that “many” may be meant

speaker: ARRAGON
text: By the fool multitude that choose by show,

speaker: ARRAGON
text: Not learning more than the fond eye doth teach,

speaker: ARRAGON
text: Which pries not to th’ interior, but like the martlet

speaker: ARRAGON
text: Builds in the weather on the outward wall,

speaker: ARRAGON
text: Even in the force and road of casualty.

speaker: ARRAGON
text: I will not choose what many men desire,

speaker: ARRAGON
text: Because I will not jump with common spirits

speaker: ARRAGON
text: And rank me with the barbarous multitudes.

speaker: ARRAGON
text: Why, then, to thee, thou silver treasure house.

speaker: ARRAGON
text: Tell me once more what title thou dost bear.

speaker: ARRAGON
text: “Who chooseth me shall get as much as he deserves.”

speaker: ARRAGON
text: And well said, too; for who shall go about

speaker: ARRAGON
text: To cozen fortune and be honorable

speaker: ARRAGON
text: Without the stamp of merit? Let none presume

speaker: ARRAGON
text: To wear an undeservèd dignity.

speaker: ARRAGON
text: O, that estates, degrees, and offices

speaker: ARRAGON
text: Were not derived corruptly, and that clear honor

speaker: ARRAGON
text: Were purchased by the merit of the wearer!

speaker: ARRAGON
text: How many then should cover that stand bare?

speaker: ARRAGON
text: How many be commanded that command?

speaker: ARRAGON
text: How much low peasantry would then be gleaned

speaker: ARRAGON
text: From the true seed of honor? And how much honor

speaker: ARRAGON
text: Picked from the chaff and ruin of the times,

speaker: ARRAGON
text: To be new varnished? Well, but to my choice.

speaker: ARRAGON
text: “Who chooseth me shall get as much as he deserves.”

speaker: ARRAGON
text: I will assume desert. Give me a key for this,

speaker: ARRAGON
text: And instantly unlock my fortunes here.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Too long a pause for that which you find there.

speaker: ARRAGON
text: What’s here? The portrait of a blinking idiot

speaker: ARRAGON
text: Presenting me a schedule! I will read it.—

speaker: ARRAGON
text: How much unlike art thou to Portia!

speaker: ARRAGON
text: How much unlike my hopes and my deservings.

speaker: ARRAGON
text: “Who chooseth me shall have as much as he deserves”?

speaker: ARRAGON
text: Did I deserve no more than a fool’s head?

speaker: ARRAGON
text: Is that my prize? Are my deserts no better?

speaker: PORTIA
text: To offend and judge are distinct offices

speaker: PORTIA
text: And of opposèd natures.

speaker: ARRAGON
text: What is here?

speaker: ARRAGON
text: The fire seven times tried this;

speaker: ARRAGON
text: Seven times tried that judgment is

speaker: ARRAGON
text: That did never choose amiss.

speaker: ARRAGON
text: Some there be that shadows kiss;

speaker: ARRAGON
text: Such have but a shadow’s bliss.

speaker: ARRAGON
text: There be fools alive, iwis,

speaker: ARRAGON
text: Silvered o’er—and so was this.

speaker: ARRAGON
text: Take what wife you will to bed,

speaker: ARRAGON
text: I will ever be your head.

speaker: ARRAGON
text: So begone; you are sped.

speaker: ARRAGON
text: Still more fool I shall appear

speaker: ARRAGON
text: By the time I linger here.

speaker: ARRAGON
text: With one fool’s head I came to woo,

speaker: ARRAGON
text: But I go away with two.

speaker: ARRAGON
text: Sweet, adieu. I’ll keep my oath,

speaker: ARRAGON
text: Patiently to bear my wroth.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Thus hath the candle singed the moth.

speaker: PORTIA
text: O, these deliberate fools, when they do choose,

speaker: PORTIA
text: They have the wisdom by their wit to lose.

speaker: NERISSA
text: The ancient saying is no heresy:

speaker: NERISSA
text: Hanging and wiving goes by destiny.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Come, draw the curtain, Nerissa.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Where is my lady?

speaker: PORTIA
text: Here. What would my lord?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Madam, there is alighted at your gate

speaker: MESSENGER
text: A young Venetian, one that comes before

speaker: MESSENGER
text: To signify th’ approaching of his lord,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: From whom he bringeth sensible regreets;

speaker: MESSENGER
text: To wit (besides commends and courteous breath),

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Gifts of rich value; yet I have not seen

speaker: MESSENGER
text: So likely an ambassador of love.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: A day in April never came so sweet,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: To show how costly summer was at hand,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: As this fore-spurrer comes before his lord.

speaker: PORTIA
text: No more, I pray thee. I am half afeard

speaker: PORTIA
text: Thou wilt say anon he is some kin to thee,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Thou spend’st such high-day wit in praising him!

speaker: PORTIA
text: Come, come, Nerissa, for I long to see

speaker: PORTIA
text: Quick Cupid’s post that comes so mannerly.

speaker: NERISSA
text: Bassanio, Lord Love, if thy will it be!
